text: Kickoff call with Priya Sharma. They are evaluating our ERP platform and seem very interested in the inventory module.
Priya shared their requirements. They need GST invoicing, purchase order tracking, and vendor management.
Product demo went well. Priya said the dashboard and reporting features exceeded their expectations.
Sent the proposal. They promised to review and revert with a decision by June 30.
Priya confirmed they are going ahead. They promised to send the signed contract by July 5.
Received signed contract! They promised to arrange the initial payment of INR 2,50,000 by July 10.
Initial meeting with Vikram Mehta. They are unhappy with their current vendor and want to switch.
Vikram shared a list of 15 pain points with their current system. Very detailed requirements.
Product demo to their entire procurement team. Feedback was mostly positive but they had concerns about migration complexity.
Follow-up call. Vikram said they need a detailed migration plan before they can commit.
Shared migration roadmap. They promised to evaluate internally and respond by June 20.
Vikram called. They have decided to go with a competitor. Relationship ended.
Onboarding completed. They promised to run parallel testing for 2 weeks before full cutover.
Testing is going well. Minor UI issues reported but nothing blocking.
Full cutover done! System is live. They are very happy with the smooth transition.
First monthly review. Everything running well. They mentioned they are considering adding the analytics add-on.
Upgrade discussion call. They want the analytics module. Promised to send the PO by May 30.
Received PO for analytics add-on worth INR 1,80,000. Implementation scheduled.
Analytics module deployed. They are thrilled with the new dashboards. Very positive feedback overall.
Cold inbound inquiry from their COO. They heard about us through a LinkedIn post and want a demo.
Discovery call. They have 300+ suppliers and need automated PO matching and discrepancy detection.
Scheduled a workshop for requirements gathering. They promised to send their integration specs by June 30.
Received integration specs. They use SAP for finance and a custom system for procurement.
Specs reviewed by engineering. Feasible with some custom API work. Promised to share effort estimate by July 7.
Signed up for the free trial. They manage last-mile delivery for 3 cities.
Noticed they have not logged in after the first week. Sent a check-in email.
Still no activity. Followed up with a call. They said they got busy but will try again.
Trial period ended. They did not convert. Marked as inactive prospect.
Onboarding call. They supply corrugated boxes and have been in business for 12 years.
Received their rate card and samples. Quality is good. They promised to deliver first bulk order by May 25.
Placed PO-201 for 5000 units. They confirmed the order and promised delivery by June 5.
Order update: 3000 units ready for dispatch. Balance delayed due to raw material shortage.
Partial delivery received (3000 units). Remaining 2000 units promised by June 20.
Remaining units still not delivered. Account manager is not responding to calls. Very concerning.
Escalated to their regional head. They promised to complete the order by July 5 or face penalties.
Initial meeting. They supply industrial solvents and cleaning agents. Very professional team.
Audited their manufacturing plant. Found excellent quality control processes and safety standards.
Approved as vendor. Placed first PO-301 for 200 liters of cleaning solvent. They promised delivery by July 1.
Delivery received on time. Product quality matches specification. Very satisfied.
Placed second PO-302 for 500 liters. They offered a 5% volume discount. Promised delivery by July 20.
Long-term partner review meeting. Overall relationship is strong and consistent.
Received quarterly supply report. On-time delivery rate is 97%. Impressive reliability.
Discussed the upcoming peak season. They committed to maintaining the same quality and delivery timelines.
Placed bulk PO-401 for Q3 requirements. They gave us a 8% early-order discount. Promised delivery in weekly batches starting July.
First weekly batch arrived ahead of schedule. Trustworthy as always.
Second batch delivered on time. Quality check passed with zero defects.
New vendor onboarding. They provide managed IT services and cloud infrastructure support.
Rahul from Nexus joined our architecture review call. Very knowledgeable about AWS and Azure.
Signed a 6-month contract for cloud monitoring services starting July 1.
Service went live. They are proactively monitoring our infrastructure. First week has been smooth.
Started partnership for ocean freight services. Good rates for our shipping volume.
First shipment handled well. Everything on time and within budget.
Second shipment delayed by 3 days. They blamed port congestion.
Third shipment delayed again. Customer complaints are piling up.
Another delay. This is becoming a pattern. We are losing trust.
Missed a critical shipment window. Lost a major client because of this.
Issued formal warning. They promised improvement but we are skeptical.
No improvement. Decision made to terminate the contract effective immediately.
Partnership discussion initiated. They are a leading digital transformation consultancy.
Signed the partnership MoU. They will resell our ERP platform to their clients in the ASEAN region.
Training session for their sales team completed. They are enthusiastic about the product.
They have their first 3 prospects lined up for demos next month. Promising start.
Shared marketing collateral and case studies. They promised to run a webinar featuring our platform by July end.
Just a single interaction note to test entities with minimal data.
Quarterly check-in call. Everything is fine on their end.
Follow-up meeting. No commitments made. Just status updates.
Planning session. They promised to finalize next quarter roadmap by September 15.
They confirmed the roadmap review meeting is scheduled for August 10.
Great initial meeting but they had some concerns about pricing.
Follow-up went well but they are still negotiating on the annual maintenance costs.
Frustrated with response times but happy with the product quality.
They promised to deliver samples by May 10.
Samples received and approved. Promise fulfilled.
They promised to send the bulk order by June 15.
Bulk order arrived with slight delay but complete. Quality is acceptable.
They promised to share the new catalog by July 5.
Kickoff meeting. They promised to share requirements by June 10 and sign NDA by June 12.
Received requirements document but NDA is still pending.
NDA signed and also promised to schedule the technical demo by June 25.
Technical demo scheduled for July 2. They promised to have their IT team available.
